1.q: The transgenic plants showed strong scavenging ability of reactive oxygen species(ROS), which was attributed to the enhancing of the content of antioxidant enzymes like superoxide dismutase (SOD) and catalase (CAT).

2.q: The transgenic plants showed strong scavenging ability of reactive oxygen species(ROS), which was attributed to the enhancing of the content of antioxidant enzymes like superoxide dismutase (SOD) and catalase (CAT).

3.q: Rt and Ralt in PpAOX OX plants were higher than that in WT P. patens under both normal growth and salt stress conditions... increased expression of PpAOX leads to enhanced Alt respiration.

5.q: we have provided experimental evidence demonstrating much lower levels of SOD, CAT, and POD activity in ppakr1a plants than in WT plants under salt stress, leading to significant increases in the concentrations of H2O2 in ppakr1a plants.

6.q: overexpression of PpCKX1 caused many phenotypic changes at different developmental stages... PpCKX1-overexpressor plants show enhanced dehydration and salt stress tolerance... reduced CKs confer increased rhizoid growth and enhanced dehydration and salt stress tolerance.

7.q: the H2O2 accumulation in PpAOX OX plants was lower than that in WT under salt stress condition... ROS accumulation in PpAOX OX plants was only half of that in WT upon salt exposure.

8.q: Overexpression of PpAOX disturbed redox homeostasis in chloroplasts... photosynthetic activity in PpAOX OX plants was also lower compared with that in WT.

9.q: using different protein–protein interaction methods we show interaction between Pp3c6_1080V3.1 and the previously characterised putative heterogenous nuclear ribonucleoprotein PpLIF2L1 in protonemata

10.q: two pairs of paralogous CKX proteins, PpCKX1/PpCKX4 and PpCKX2/PpCKX5, respectively, share about 81% and 74% identity, suggesting a relatively recent divergence in each protein pair.

11.q: two pairs of paralogous CKX proteins, PpCKX1/PpCKX4 and PpCKX2/PpCKX5, respectively, share about 81% and 74% identity, suggesting a relatively recent divergence in each protein pair.

12.q: PpHsp16.4 is encoded by two identical genes arranged in tandem in the genome... suggesting their origin from a single ancestral gene that most likely had undergone recent events of tandem duplication and inversion.

16.q: The relative expression levels of PpABI3, PpABI5, and PpPP2C in PpSARKg plants increased at least 2-fold during salinity treatment and recovery than in wild type.

17.q: The relative expression levels of PpABI3, PpABI5, and PpPP2C in PpSARKg plants increased at least 2-fold during salinity treatment and recovery than in wild type.

18.q: The relative expression levels of PpABI3, PpABI5, and PpPP2C in PpSARKg plants increased at least 2-fold during salinity treatment and recovery than in wild type.

19.q: The downstream functional gene (PpLEA) was also accumulated in PpSARKg plants.

20.q: ABA signaling components SUCROSE NON-FERMENTING 1-RELATED PROTEIN KINASE 2 (PpSnRK2) and ABA INSENSITIVE 3 (PpABI3) play roles as an essential and promotive factor, respectively, in regulating ABA-induced suppression of Dendra2 diffusion between cells (ASD).
These results suggest PpSARK took part in the ABA pathway and possibly regulates salt tolerance and senescence via an ABA-related pathway.

21.q: disruption of PpSnRK2 resulted in defective ASD onset itself... intercellular Dendra2 diffusion in the ProEF1α:D2/ppsnrk2qko was observable irrespective of the presence or absence of ABA treatment. This indicates that PpSnRK2 is an indispensable factor for ASD.

22.q: More ABA-signaling-related mRNAs (PpABI3, PpABI5, and PpPP2C) were significantly accumulated in PpSARKg plants than in wild type, indicating plants obtained higher salt stress tolerance related to ABA signaling.

23.q: the impaired Na+ exclusion in ena1 plants led to an approximately 40% decrease in growth

24.q: PpCIPK1 completely rescued the fresh weight and primary root growth of sos2-1 to WT level in Pro35S::PpCIPK1/sos2-1 plants, supporting functional conservation with AtSOS2 in the SOS pathway.

25.q: Comparison of wild-type, and PpDsos1 and PpDena1/PpDsos1 mutant lines revealed that PpSOS1 is crucial for Na+ efﬂux

26.q: Comparison of wild-type, and PpDsos1 and PpDena1/PpDsos1 mutant lines revealed that PpSOS1 is crucial for Na+ efﬂux

27.q: PpCIPK1 KO plants accumulated higher level of Na+ but lower level of K+ compared with WT plants with salt treatment...PpCIPK1 is involved in salt tolerance in P. patens possibly through ionic homeostasis modulation.

28.q: PpDNMT2 possibly exists in complex with CuZn-SODs in vivo and the two proteins also directly interact in the yeast nucleus as observed by yeast two-hybrid assay.

29.q: PpDNMT2 possibly exists in complex with CuZn-SODs in vivo and the two proteins also directly interact in the yeast nucleus as observed by yeast two-hybrid assay.

30.q: PpcHsp70-1 PpcHsp70-2 ... Duplication T; cHsp70-1 and cHsp70-2 were linked tail-to-tail on Chr3.

31.q: PpcHsp70-3 PpcHsp70-10 0.04 0.84 0.05 W; Synteny involving cHsp70-3 and cHsp70-10 linked homologues.

32.q: PpcHsp70-5 PpcHsp70-9 0.15 0.71 0.21 W; cHsp70-5 on Chr 7 was also linked segmentally to cHsp70-9 on Chr 11.

33.q: PpcHsp70-6 PpcHsp70-8 0.01 1.4 0.01 W; large blocks of synteny between Chr7 and 11 including cHsp70-6/cHsp70-8.

34.q: mtHsp70-2 and mtHsp70-3 were found in tail-to-tail orientation on Chr1. These genes likely arose from local gene duplication.

35.q: PpSOS1 is crucial for Na+ efflux and that the PpDsos1 line, and especially the PpDena1/PpDsos1 lines, showed excessive Na+ accumulation and Na+-triggered cell death.

36.q: PpHsp16.4 was strongly induced by treatment with ABA, SA and DTT and after exposure of plants to heat, strong light, and salt or osmotic stress.

37.q: Based on the functional characterization of nhad mutants, we also discuss the putative role of NHAD transporters in Na+ homeostasis and osmoregulation of this organelle.

38.q: targeted disruption of Physcomitrella ABI1-related gene (PpABI1A) resulted in altered ABA sensitivity and abiotic stress tolerance of Physcomitrella.

39.q: ABA signaling components SUCROSE NON-FERMENTING 1-RELATED PROTEIN KINASE 2 (PpSnRK2) and ABA INSENSITIVE 3 (PpABI3) play roles as an essential and promotive factor, respectively, in regulating ABA-induced suppression of Dendra2 diffusion between cells (ASD).

40.q: At 100 mm NaCl, wild-type plants were able to maintain a higher K+-to-Na+ ratio than the PpENA1 (ena1) knockout gene

41.q: we analysed the enzyme activity of PpAKR1A and the concentrations of MG and confirmed that PpAKR1A catalysed the reduction of MG; PpAKR1A exhibited the highest catalytic efficiency with MG.

42.q: there might exist functional conservation of SOS pathway across the plant kingdom...PpCIPK1 could completely rescue the salt-sensitive phenotype of sos2-1 to WT level in Arabidopsis.
